title: poa nemoralis var. marginata
intro: Poa nemoralis var. marginata, also known as the Wood Meadow-grass, is a type of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its slender stems and yellow-green leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Wood Meadow-grass include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in herbal infusions to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
